title.purpose: Section titles, modal titles, card names.

body.purpose: Task titles, form values, comments, general content.

label.purpose: Field labels, dsc-label, table headers, sidebar section labels — always uppercase and tracked.

shadows.name: shadow
shadows.purpose: Default resting elevation for cards and stat tiles.

shadows.name: shadow-md
shadows.purpose: Hover/interactive elevation for clickable cards and buttons.

shadows.name: hero-shadow
shadows.purpose: Exclusive to .dash-stat-card--done, the one named burgundy-gradient hero exception.

motion.name: transition-default
motion.purpose: Default state-change transition across buttons, nav items, inputs.

motion.name: ease-progress
motion.purpose: Progress bar / count-up / donut fill animations.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Standard call-to-action button, solid burgundy fill.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1.5px solid transparent; background: #8E1B3B; color: #fff; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; transition: background 0.17s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #B4234C; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #8E1B3B; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Hero CTA Button
components.description: The single prominent top-bar CTA (e.g. 'Новая задача'), gradient fill + glow — a sanctioned secondary decorative use of burgundy.
components.css: .ds-btn-hero { padding: 10px 18px; border-radius: 20px; background: linear-gradient(140deg, #8E1B3B, #B4234C); color: #fff; font-size: 13.5px; font-weight: 700; box-shadow: 0 0 16px -2px rgba(142,27,59,.35), 0 6px 18px -6px #8E1B3B; transition: transform 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease; } .ds-btn-hero:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); box-shadow: 0 0 24px -2px rgba(142,27,59,.5), 0 10px 22px -6px #8E1B3B; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Base surface card used across dashboard, tasks, projects.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #fff; border-radius: 20px; border: 1px solid rgba(17,17,26,.07); padding: 20px; box-shadow: 0 1px 3px rgba(17,17,26,.06), 0 1px 2px rgba(17,17,26,.04); }

components.name: Done Stat Card (hero exception)
components.description: The dashboard's one named decorative-gradient exception — burgundy gradient, blurred orb decorations, white text.
components.html:
Выполнено
48%
components.css: .ds-done-card { position: relative; overflow: hidden; border-radius: 20px; padding: 20px 22px; background: linear-gradient(145deg, #8E1B3B 0%, #B4234C 100%); color: #fff; box-shadow: 0 8px 24px -8px rgba(142,27,59,.5); } .ds-orb { position: absolute; border-radius: 50%; pointer-events: none; z-index: 0; } .ds-orb-1 { width: 130px; height: 130px; right: 30px; top: -10px; background: rgba(255,255,255,.30); filter: blur(22px); } .ds-orb-2 { width: 100px; height: 100px; left: 10px; bottom: -10px; background: rgba(255,170,170,.32); filter: blur(28px); } .ds-label, .ds-value { position: relative; z-index: 1; } .ds-label { font-size: 10.5px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: rgba(255,255,255,.70); } .ds-value { font-size: 46px; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: -0.03em; }

components.name: Text Field
components.css: .ds-field-label { display: block; font-size: 11.5px; font-weight: 700; color: #6E6E7C; margin-bottom: 6px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; } .ds-field-input { width: 100%; padding: 9px 12px; border: 1.5px solid rgba(17,17,26,.07); border-radius: 8px; font-size: 13.5px; outline: none; background: #fff; color: #16161D; transition: border-color 0.17s ease, box-shadow 0.17s ease; } .ds-field-input:focus { border-color: #8E1B3B; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(142,27,59,.08); }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Sidebar navigation item — no border-left indicator in the current system, wash + color-shift only.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 10px; color: #6E6E7C; font-size: 13.5px; font-weight: 600; transition: background 0.17s ease, color 0.17s ease; cursor: pointer; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: #F4F4F7; color: #16161D; } .ds-nav-item--active { background: rgba(142,27,59,.10); color: #8E1B3B; font-weight: 700; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.css: .ds-status-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; padding: 3px 9px; border-radius: 20px; font-size: 11.5px; font-weight: 600; } .ds-status-badge--done { background: #ECFDF5; color: #065F46; } .ds-status-badge--new { background: #EFF6FF; color: #1D4ED8; } .ds-status-badge--in-progress { background: rgba(217,130,43,.12); color: #92400E; } .ds-status-badge--pending-review { background: #f3f0ff; color: #6d28d9; }

narrative.overview: TeamTask is a calm, single-surface workspace for an agency's daily task and project accounting. Unlike the earlier dark-sidebar-vs-light-canvas version of this system, the current build is unified neutral: sidebar, topbar, and content all sit on the same light (or, in dark theme, the same near-black) base — there is no structural light/dark split between navigation and content anymore. Burgundy (#8E1B3B) is the one committed brand color, and it now carries two roles rather than one: the reserved action/active-state color everywhere else in the system, and a single named hero exception — the dashboard's 'Выполнено' (Done) stat tile, which is intentionally rendered as a full burgundy gradient with soft blur orbs, distinguishing the primary completion metric from its three plain-white sibling tiles. This system explicitly rejects toy interfaces, emoji-as-decoration, decorative gradients used indiscriminately, glassmorphism-for-its-own-sake, and animation that exists for spectacle rather than feedback — the one hero-card gradient is the sanctioned exception to 'no decorative gradients,' not a loophole to reuse elsewhere.
keyCharacteristics: Unified neutral surface (no dark/light structural split between sidebar and canvas)
One committed brand color (burgundy) for actions, active state, and exactly one named hero metric
A real, fully-tokenized dark theme alongside light — not an afterthought
Ambient, colorless shadows for elevation — never tinted, never theatrical (except the one hero card)
Restrained, functional motion; the hero-card blur orbs are static decoration, not motion

rules.name: The One Hero Exception Rule
rules.body: Burgundy appears as decorative gradient fill in exactly one place system-wide: the dashboard's 'Выполнено' stat card (.dash-stat-card--done). Every other surface follows the One Accent Rule. Never copy the gradient-fill treatment onto another card or tile.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule (everywhere else)
rules.body: Outside the one named exception, burgundy appears only at decision points: primary buttons, active nav/tab/filter state, focus rings, links.

rules.name: The Semantic-Only Blue Rule
rules.body: Accent blue (#3B82F6) is reserved exclusively for the 'new' task status.

rules.name: The Weight-Not-Style Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy is built by weight and size shifts within one family, never by switching fonts or adding italics.

rules.name: The Neutral-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadows are colorless (tinted toward ink, not toward brand) everywhere except the one named hero exception.

rules.name: The Data-Bound Border Rule
rules.body: A colored border-left is legitimate exactly when its color is computed from data (urgency, category, project, task type) and communicates that one fact at a glance. Confirmed uses: task-card urgency, content-plan chips, workload project-picker buttons, finance summary cards. Illegitimate when static/decorative and unrelated to data.

dos: Do keep burgundy (#8E1B3B) as the accent for actions and active states everywhere except the two named exceptions (Done stat card, primary CTA button).
Do treat the Done stat card's gradient-plus-blur-orb treatment as a one-off hero component — never copy it onto another card or tile.
Do use colorless ambient shadows for elevation everywhere except the Done card's burgundy-tinted shadow.
Do build hierarchy through Gotham's weight range, not through switching typefaces.
Do keep status color semantics fixed: green = done/positive, amber = pending/soon, red = overdue/destructive, blue = new-status only, violet = pending_review.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion on every transition and animation (WCAG AA per PRODUCT.md).
Do maintain full light/dark theme parity — every new token needs both a light and [data-theme="dark"] value.
donts: Don't introduce toy-app visual language: no emoji-as-decoration, no playful illustration, no mascot elements.
Don't add a colored border-left that is hardcoded/static rather than data-bound — legitimate uses (task urgency, category, project color) are fine; decorative ones aren't.
Don't extend decorative gradient fills beyond the two named exceptions (Done stat card, .btn-blue primary CTA).
Don't add bounce, elastic, or overshoot easing to any transition.
Don't introduce a second accent color competing with burgundy; map new semantic needs to the existing green/amber/red/blue/violet set first.
Don't let body or placeholder text drop below 4.5:1 contrast in either theme — verify dark-theme contrast separately.
